{{Quote|Looks like our informant was being candid, team… We most definitely have the beginnings of a secret Imperial base over here!|F4-MF, on Vatleria|<ref name="Rebel Four" />}}
'''Vatleria''' was a dark and [[Water/Legends|foggy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Vatleria system/Legends|Vatleria system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Long abandoned by its inhabitants, the world eventually became the site of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] base in its [[stone ruins]]. By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the base was [[Mission to Vatleria|assaulted]] by the [[Rebel Four]], an [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] team, but they were defeated by machinations of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

==Description==
Vatleria was a dying terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] covered in [[Water/Legends|fog]] and darkness, located near more prosperous [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] worlds.<ref name="Rebel Four" /> The planet was within its [[Vatleria system/Legends|eponymous star system]],<ref name="Atlas" /> which itself was located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> inside [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Region" />

==History==
{{Quote|Surely you jest, Lord Vader. A swift defeat such as this is something that the Empire can be proud of… ''[choking]''<nowiki />|An Imperial officer, interrupted by being choked by Darth Vader|<ref name="Rebel Four" />}}
[[File:Skirmish_on_Vatleria_SWT9.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Rebel Four attack Imperials on Vatleria.]]
Decaying conditions on Vatleria led to its abandonment by its inhabitants long before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="Rebel Four" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date">Following the [[Mission to Vatleria|mission]] to Vatleria in "[[The Rebel Four]]," the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ends with the statement that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] will fulfill his wish for [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] with a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] soon, referencing his [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] with [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] during the [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|Rescue of Princess Leia]] in {{Film|IV}}, which takes place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Empire began to establish a base within Vatleria's [[stone ruins]] due to the world's proximity to successful colonies, taking advantage of the planet's dark and foggy atmosphere. An [[Unidentified informant|informant]] passed along information regarding the base to the [[Rebel Four]], a team of Alliance operatives that included [[F4-MF]], [[Falaem Onn]], [[Grimgrim]], and [[Soo Rcharrz]]. The four [[Mission to Vatleria|travelled]] to the ruins, where F4-MF observed Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] patrolling the area outside their base.<ref name="Rebel Four" />

The Rebel Four charged into the base, attacking the stormtroopers within. However, the Rebels were caught in traps activated by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who was observing them through a screen. [[Rope/Legends|Ropes]] emerged that bound each of the Rebel Four and pulled them into [[Death/Legends|death]] chambers, where they were each dispatched gruesomely. Afterward, Vader lamented to an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Vatleria)|Imperial officer]] that he had not been challenged and wished to fight a Jedi again. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] then [[Telekinesis/Legends|choked]] the [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] when they attempted to highlight the Empire's victory.<ref name="Rebel Four" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vatleria appeared in "[[The Rebel Four]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] written and illustrated by [[Jay Stephens]] and published in the [[Star Wars Tales 9|ninth issue]] of the anthology series ''[[Star Wars Tales]]''<ref name="Rebel Four" /> by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[October 10]], [[2001]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/00-458/Star-Wars-Tales-9-photo-cover|text=Star Wars Tales 9}}</ref> The planet's name is an anagram of [[Wikipedia:Latveria|Latveria]], the homeland of the [[Marvel Comics]] [[Wikipedia:Fantastic Four|Fantastic Four]] supervillain [[Wikipedia:Doctor Doom|Doctor Doom]].<ref name="Latveria">{{Marvel|url=characters/doctor-doom-victor-von-doom/in-comics|text=Doctor Doom (Victor von Doom)}}</ref> In spite of the comic's [[Canon|non-canonical]] status within the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] continuity,<ref name="Rebel Four" /> the Vatleria system was still mentioned in the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]], which placed the system, and therefore Vatleria, in grid square T-9.<ref name="Atlas" />
